data.title: Jeffs’ Brands and Impact Acquisitions gets TSX Venture Exchange nod for Fort Products’ merger
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR)  announced Friday that the TSX Venture Exchange has approved the previously disclosed acquisition by Impact Acquisitions of 100% of the equity in Fort Products, a U.K.-based wholly owned subsidiary of Jeffs’ Brands.
  • In return, Jeffs’ Brands will receive an initial 75.02% stake in Impact, with the potential to increase to 83.29% based on the achievement of certain milestones.
  • Upon completion of the proposed transaction, Impact will issue 100,000,000 common shares to the company at a deemed price of C$0.171246 per share, representing a total implied consideration of approximately C$17.1 million.

data.title: Centrus Energy wins DoE contract extension to continue HALEU production
data.description:
Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +3.7% pre-market Friday after saying the U.S. Department of Energy exercised an option to extend its contract for producing high-assay, low-enriched uranium through June 30, 2026; the DoE has additional options for continued production as many as eight additional years.

The one-year extension, valued at ~$110M, continues the partnership the DoE launched with Centrus (NYSE:LEU) in 2019 to restore U.S. ability to enrich uranium and provide a source of HALEU.

Centrus (LEU) completed Phase 1 of a three-phase contract in late 2023 by launching enrichment operations and delivering 20 kg of HALEU, and Phase 2, which concludes June 30, 2025, the company is producing an additional 900 kg of HALEU for the DoE's use.

The DoE amended the contract this month, splitting the first three-year extension period into a one-year option followed by a two-year option; the department has exercised the first of these options, kicking off Phase 3I with additional HALEU production through June 30, 2026.

The remaining options in the contract would, if exercised, provide for up to eight additional years of production beyond the current extension, the company said.

data.title: Aflac reports cybersecurity incident
data.description:
Health insurer Aflac (NYSE:AFL) announced on Friday the identification of "suspicious activity" in its U.S. network on June 12.

"We promptly initiated our cyber incident response protocols and stopped the intrusion within hours," said the company in a statement.

Aflac said the investigation remains in its early stages, and preliminary findings indicate that the unauthorized party used social engineering tactics to gain access to the company's network. The insurer has commenced a review of the potentially impacted files.

The company said it is unable to determine the total number of affected individuals until the review is completed.

The insurer said the cybersecurity attack "was caused by a sophisticated cybercrime group", and "was part of a cybercrime campaign against the insurance industry".

data.title: Bakkt rises on $25M securities purchase agreement news
data.description:
Bakkt Holdings (NYSE:BKKT) was rising as the crypto solutions provider announced a $25M securities purchase agreement with the Cayman Islands exempt limited company, YA II PN.

Shares were +4.89% Friday pre-market to $13.31.

The investor YA II PN is set to acquire convertible debenture from Bakkt for $23.75M as part of the private placement. The private placement closed on June 18.

The convertible debenture, expected to mature on the first anniversary of the closing date, accrues interest at an annual rate equal to 0%, which can increase to a rate of 18% upon the occurrence of default.

The net proceeds are intended to be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: American Express, Dell, Walt Disney, Philip Morris, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from First Farmers Financial (OTCQX:FFMR) and Acme United (ACU) as well as declarations from companies such as American Express (NYSE:AXP), and Dell (NYSE:DELL).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Walt Disney (DIS) and Philip Morris (PM) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

First Farmers Financial (OTCQX:FFMR)

  • Dividend declared: June 17
  • New dividend amount: $0.50
  • Previous amount: $0.49
  • Increase: 2%

Acme United (ACU)

  • Dividend declared: June 18
  • New dividend amount: $0.16
  • Previous amount: $0.15
  • Increase: 6.7%

CNB Community Bancorp (OTCQX:CNBB)

  • Dividend declared: June 18
  • New dividend amount: $0.31
  • Previous amount: $0.30
  • Increase: 3.3%

Dividend Declarations

Dell (NYSE:DELL)

Bristol-Myers Squibb Company (BMY)

U.S. Bancorp (USB)

  • Dividend declared: June 18

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Walt Disney (DIS)

  • Payout date: July 23

Humana (HUM)

  • Ex-dividend date: June 27
  • Payout date: July 25

Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP)

Philip Morris (PM)

data.title: Kroger Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.03, revenue of $45.12B misses by $190M
data.description:
• Kroger press release (NYSE:KR): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.49 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $45.12B (-0.4% Y/Y) misses by $190M.
  • Total company sales were $45.1 billion in the first quarter compared to $45.3 billion for the same period last year, which included $917 million from Kroger Specialty Pharmacy sales. Excluding fuel, Kroger Specialty Pharmacy and adjustment items, sales increased 3.7% compared to the same period last year.
  • eCommerce sales increased 15%
  • Identical Sales without fuel increased 3.2%

  • Full-Year 2025 Guidance*

    Updated

    • Identical Sales without fuel of 2.25% – 3.25%

    Reaffirmed

    • Adjusted FIFO Operating Profit of $4.7 – $4.9 billion
    • Adjusted net earnings per diluted share of $4.60 – $4.80
    • Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $2.8 – $3.0 billion
    • Capital expenditures of $3.6 – $3.8 billion
    • Adjusted effective tax rate of 23%
